from pyspark.sql import functions as F

CASE_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/LLAttr/Case_95498"

case_ref = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", "|")
        .option("multiLine", True)          
        .option("quote", '"')               # handle quoted fields
        .option("escape", '"')              # handle escaped quotes
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")       # don’t fail on bad rows
        .option("inferSchema", False)       # safer for messy exports
        .load(CASE_FILE)
)

display(case_ref.limit(10))
print("case_ref rows:", case_ref.count())

all_lpas = (
   case_ref
   .select(
            F.col("`76`").alias("LPA Code"),
            F.col("`77`").alias("LPA Name")
            )
   .distinct()
   )
display(all_lpas.limit(10))
print("all_lpas rows:", all_lpas.count())
